data.content:
### GET Revenue Recognition By Invoice Report

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/revenue-recognition/api

This API report provides detailed debit and credit entries broken down by invoice, enabling comprehensive revenue recognition accounting. It outlines the financial impact of transactions on various accounts.

```APIDOC
## GET /reports/revenue_recognition.debit_credit_by_invoice.1

### Description
This report type provides detailed debit and credit entries for revenue recognition, organized by invoice. It helps track financial changes associated with invoices, invoice items, and other Stripe objects.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`/reports/revenue_recognition.debit_credit_by_invoice.1`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None explicitly documented for this report type, but standard filtering/pagination parameters may apply in an API context)

#### Request Body
(Not applicable for a GET request to retrieve a report)

### Request Example
(Not applicable for a simple GET request for a report)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
This report returns an array of debit and credit entries, each with the following fields:

- **accounting_period** (string) - Required - The accounting period.
- **open_accounting_period** (boolean) - Required - Indicates if the accounting period is open; entries in open periods are subject to change.
- **currency** (string) - Required - Three-letter [ISO code for the currency](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies.md) of the amount.
- **transaction_model_id** (string) - Required - The model in Stripe associated with this change - either an invoice line item, invoice, invoiceitem, charge, or etc.
- **debit** (string) - Required - The debited account.
- **credit** (string) - Required - The credited account.
- **booked_date** (string) - Required - The date that the ledger entry is added to the books.
- **amount** (number) - Required - Amount change, expressed in major units of the currency (for example, dollars for USD, or pesos for MXN).
- **debit_gl_code** (string, nullable) - Optional - The debited general ledger code.
- **credit_gl_code** (string, nullable) - Optional - The credited general ledger code.
- **invoice_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The invoice associated with this change. Standalone charges or invoice items not associated with an invoice are `null`.
- **invoice_line_item_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The ID of the invoice line_item.
- **invoice_item_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The ID of the invoice item.
- **invoice_number** (string, nullable) - Optional - The customer unique number associated with the invoice.
- **subscription_item_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The ID of the subscription_item.
- **price_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The price associated with this change. Standalone charges or invoice items not associated with a price are `null`.
- **product_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The product associated with this price.
- **customer_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The customer associated with this change.
- **subscription_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The subscription associated with this change.
- **charge_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The charge associated with this change.
- **refund_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The refund associated with this change.
- **dispute_id** (string, nullable) - Optional - The dispute associated with this change.
- **presentment_currency** (string, nullable) - Optional - The presentment (customer facing) currency of the transaction.
- **presentment_amount** (number, nullable) - Optional - The presentment (customer facing) amount.

#### Response Example
```json
[
  {
    "accounting_period": "2023-Q4",
    "open_accounting_period": false,
    "currency": "USD",
    "transaction_model_id": "ii_123abc",
    "debit": "Accounts Receivable",
    "credit": "Sales Revenue",
    "booked_date": "2023-12-01T00:00:00Z",
    "amount": 100.00,
    "debit_gl_code": "1200",
    "credit_gl_code": "4000",
    "invoice_id": "in_xyz789",
    "invoice_line_item_id": "ili_def456",
    "invoice_item_id": "ii_123abc",
    "invoice_number": "INV-0001",
    "subscription_item_id": null,
    "price_id": "price_abc",
    "product_id": "prod_123",
    "customer_id": "cus_abc",
    "subscription_id": null,
    "charge_id": "ch_ghi",
    "refund_id": null,
    "dispute_id": null,
    "presentment_currency": "USD",
    "presentment_amount": 100.00
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/reporting/report_runs

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/api/reporting/report_run/create

Creates a new object and begins running a report. This endpoint allows users to define report type and optional parameters such as interval start/end, columns, currency, and connected account to generate financial reports.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/reporting/report_runs

### Description
Creates a new object and begins running a report. This endpoint allows users to define report type and optional parameters such as interval start/end, columns, currency, and connected account to generate financial reports.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/reporting/report_runs

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
- **report_type** (string) - Required - The ID of the report type to run, such as "balance.summary.1".
- **parameters** (object) - Optional - Parameters specifying how the report should be run.
  - **parameters.columns** (array of strings) - Optional - The set of report columns to include in the report output.
  - **parameters.connected_account** (string) - Optional - Connected account ID to filter for in the report run.
  - **parameters.currency** (enum) - Optional - Currency of objects to be included in the report run.
  - **parameters.interval_end** (timestamp) - Optional - Ending timestamp of data to be included in the report run (exclusive).
  - **parameters.interval_start** (timestamp) - Optional - Starting timestamp of data to be included in the report run.
  - **parameters.payout** (string) - Optional - Payout ID by which to filter the report run.
  - **parameters.reporting_category** (string) - Optional - Category of balance transactions to be included in the report run.
  - **parameters.timezone** (string) - Optional - Defaults to `Etc/UTC`. The output timezone for all timestamps in the report.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "report_type": "balance.summary.1",
  "parameters": {
    "interval_start": 1680000000,
    "interval_end": 1680100000
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - Unique identifier for the report run.
- **object** (string) - Type of the object, "reporting.report_run".
- **created** (timestamp) - Time of creation.
- **error** (object) - Error details if any, or null.
- **livemode** (boolean) - Indicates if the report run was created in live mode.
- **parameters** (object) - The parameters used for this report run.
- **report_type** (string) - The ID of the report type that was run.
- **result** (object) - The result of the report run when completed, or null if pending/failed.
- **status** (string) - Current status of the report run (e.g., "pending", "succeeded", "failed").
- **succeeded_at** (timestamp) - Time when the report run succeeded, or null.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "frr_1MrQwrLkdIwHu7ixUov4x2b3",
  "object": "reporting.report_run",
  "created": 1680203749,
  "error": null,
  "livemode": false,
  "parameters": {
    "interval_end": 1680100000,
    "interval_start": 1680000000
  },
  "report_type": "balance.summary.1",
  "result": null,
  "status": "pending",
  "succeeded_at": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/reporting/report_runs

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/network-cost-passthrough-platforms

Initiates the creation of a new report run for a specified report type and date range. This API is used to generate financial reports for a connected Stripe account.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/reporting/report_runs

### Description
Initiates the creation of a new report run for a specified report type and date range. This API is used to generate financial reports for a connected Stripe account.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/reporting/report_runs

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **report_type** (string) - Required - The type of report to run. Example: "connect_card_payments_fees.transaction_level.1"
- **parameters** (object) - Required - Specific parameters for the report run.
    - **parameters.interval_start** (integer) - Required - The start of the date range for the report (Unix timestamp).
    - **parameters.interval_end** (integer) - Required - The end of the date range for the report (Unix timestamp).

#### Headers
- **Stripe-Account** (string) - Optional - The ID of the connected account for which the report should be run.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "report_type": "connect_card_payments_fees.transaction_level.1",
  "parameters": {
    "interval_start": 1680000000,
    "interval_end": 1680100000
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - Unique identifier for the report run.
- **object** (string) - String representing the object's type, always "reporting.report_run".
- **created** (integer) - Time at which the object was created.
- **error** (object|null) - Details of any error encountered during the report run, or null if successful.
- **livemode** (boolean) - Has the value true if the object exists in live mode or false if in test mode.
- **parameters** (object) - Parameters used to generate the report.
- **report_type** (string) - The type of report that is being run.
- **result** (object) - Details about the generated file, if the report run was successful.
    - **result.id** (string) - Unique identifier for the file.
    - **result.object** (string) - Type of the object, always "file".
    - **result.created** (integer) - Time at which the object was created.
    - **result.expires_at** (integer) - Time at which the file expires.
    - **result.filename** (string) - The file's user-friendly filename.
    - **result.links** (object) - A list of links to the file.
    - **result.purpose** (string) - The purpose of the file.
    - **result.size** (integer) - The size of the file in bytes.
    - **result.title** (string) - A title for the file.
    - **result.type** (string) - The file's type.
    - **result.url** (string) - The URL from which the file can be downloaded.
- **status** (string) - The status of the report run. Can be `pending`, `succeeded`, or `failed`.
- **succeeded_at** (integer) - Time at which the report run succeeded.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "frr_1RmMK3PHHHwdjQjw0pJ2Nsga",
  "object": "reporting.report_run",
  "created": 1752876007,
  "error": null,
  "livemode": true,
  "parameters": {
    "interval_end": 1680100000,
    "interval_start": 1680000000
  },
  "report_type": "connect_card_payments_fees.transaction_level.1",
  "result": {
    "id": "file_1RmM8wPHHHwdjQjwkHoUYySJ",
    "object": "file",
    "created": 1752875318,
    "expires_at": 1784411318,
    "filename": "frr_1RmM8sPHHHwdjQjwHgTQ3dlv.csv",
    "links": {
      "object": "list",
      "data": [],
      "has_more": false,
      "url": "/v1/file_links?file=file_1RmM8wPHHHwdjQjwkHoUYySJ"
    },
    "purpose": "finance_report_run",
    "size": 6307,
    "title": "FinanceReportRun frr_1RmM8sPHHHwdjQjwHgTQ3dlv",
    "type": "csv",
    "url": "https://files.stripe.com/v1/files/file_1RmM8wPHHHwdjQjwkHoUYySJ/contents"
  },
  "status": "succeeded",
  "succeeded_at": 1752876007
}
```
```
